input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to the modern `.runsettings` format. They mention that after upgrading MSTest packages, the settings are being ignored. This is a known migration issue - `.testsettings` files are legacy and not supported by newer MSTest versions. Let me look at their files to understand what they have.

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** in MSTest v3+. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Equivalent of TestTimeout property (per-test timeout in ms) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout in ms (replaces Properties/TestTimeout) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Assembly resolution: deploy test output to run directory -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Code Coverage data collector -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

</RunSettings>

data.content: Now update the `.csproj` to reference the new `.runsettings` file:

Here's a summary of the migration:

| `.testsettings` element | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `Properties/TestTimeout` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>` (per-method) |
| `AssemblyResolution` / deployment | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>true</MSTest>` |
| `AgentRule/DataCollectors` (coverage) | `<DataCollectionRunSettings><DataCollectors>` |

**Key points:**
- `.testsettings` is **ignored** by MSTest v3 — only `.runsettings` is supported.
- The `.csproj` now references `local.runsettings` automatically via `<RunSettingsFilePath>`.
- You can also pass it manually: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`.
- The old `local.testsettings` file can be deleted once you've verified everything works.
